entry.title: Joule in SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

In the contemporary business environment, effective and integrated supply chain planning is crucial for maintaining a resilient and agile supply chain. SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is a solution designed to streamline and optimize this process, aligning strategic, tactical, and operational plans. This cloud-based platform employs advanced analytics, machine learning, and collaboration tools to improve demand forecasting, sales and operations planning, inventory management, and overall supply chain orchestration.

The integration of Joule with SAP IBP introduces the capabilities of generative AI into the planning solution. Currently, Joule supports SAP IBP through an intuitive search functionality within the help feature, allowing users to quickly find and access relevant information.

However, Joule's support for SAP IBP will not be limited to search functionality. We are actively developing advanced planning-specific capabilities aimed at increasing user proficiency and productivity. These forthcoming features will provide users with more intuitive tools and actionable insights, ultimately elevating supply chain management. The goal is to enhance responsiveness, accuracy, and strategic decision-making within the supply chain.

Here in this blog, I am going to walk you through the steps that are required for you to activate Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning, however, the updated information can always be found on the help page. If you are just getting started with Joule for your SAP IBP, please ensure to read the SAP IBP System Registration with SAP BTP listed below and first and then followed by the blogs mentioned below.

****************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule setup:

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..)
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...) 
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain

Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning  (You are here)

  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (WIP)
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, and SAP Mobile Start
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

If you are following the blog series on activating the Joule service for IBP, here are few things that you need to consider:

We need the following user Role:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform – Global Administrator
  •  SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning Administrator

Before you start with the Joule integration, we need to do the system registration for your SAP IBP system and SAP Business Technology Platform. So let us take a look into this process first and then proceed with the Joule setup.

1. SAP IBP System Registration with SAP BTP
    a. Generate the Token for your IBP System
    b. Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP in your IBP System
2. Create SAP BTP Subaccount (Refer to Joule - Unified Setup - End-to-End Setup Guide)
3. SAP IBP Integration with Joule
    a. Check Communication Arrangements
    b. Maintain Custom Content Security Policy


Let us look into the details of integration:

1. SAP IBP System Registration with SAP BTP

a. Generate the Token for your IBP System

Login to your SAP Business Technology Platform, click on System Landscape -> click on Systems -> search for your IBP System and click on it(this is be listed and showing as auto discovered).

Image 1

On the right screen click on Get Token -> in the pop up window select All Communications Scenarios -> click on Get Token.

Image 2

Copy the Token value and close the window.

Image 3

b. Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP in your IBP System

Login to your SAP IBP System and search for Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP -> click on New -> Enter the Token Value that was generated in the previous step, enter a description and click on Create.  

Image 4

2. Create SAP BTP Subaccount (Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach)

You will need to navigate to this blog to complete the Subaccount creation process and complete the steps required.

In case you are setting up Joule only for your SAP IBP systems, you may SKIP Step 6 Related to Cloud Foundry, and Step 9 related to SAP Build Work Zone activity from the Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach as IBP does not offer the Navigation Support in the current release. In case you are trying to setup IBP in the unified approach, you may need to consider activating it as it is required for SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA Cloud scenarios.

3. SAP IBP Integration with Joule

With the release of SAP IBP 2411, the communication port SAP_COM_0882 is used for Joule, which is auto-enabled on all systems. When you run the Joule Booster, it creates a communication arrangement and communication system in your IBP system for SAP Digital Assistants Services. This communication setup should not be edited or removed, in case of any changes it will impact the Joule behavior and will stop working.

a. Verify the Communication Arrangements and Communication System

This step is only required as a verification post Joule Booster run, and no changes are required, feel free to skip this verification step.  To know more about what booster has executed, you can go to your SAP IBP system, go to Communication Arrangements -> search for SAP_COM_0882, and click on it.

Image 5

You should be able to see the following details which is important to know. The booster creates a Communication Arrangement and Communication Systems which are shown below.

In the Communication Arrangement, you will be able to see the details on Common Data with API-URL and the connected Outbound Communication and Outbound Service, which has the details related to the Joule service from the SAP BTP Subaccount.

Image 6

If you click on the Display option, you will see the Communication System setting that the booster has executed.

This confirms that the setting is good and we can go ahead with the next steps.

b. Maintain Custom Content Security Policy

This step is required to enable the Joule icon in your SAP IBP System.

To enable Joule, we need the Joule Service URL. To get the URL, you can go back to your SAP BTP Subaccount -> Click on Instance and Subscription -> Click on Joule. You should be able to see the message “The service is up and running.” You need to copy the URL as shown below.

Image 7

Now, go back to your SAP IBP System navigate to Manage Content Security Policy app -> click on Trusted Sites -> navigate to Resources Scripts (UI_RESOURCES_SCRIPTS).

Image 8

Under the Managed by Customer click on New and enter the Joule URL and Save the settings.

Image 9

Refresh your SAP IBP system to get the Joule icon active. This completes the Joule integration with the SAP IBP system.

Image 10

4. Verify your Joule Integration

Now let us go ahead and interact with Joule from your SAP IBP System. Click on the Joule Diamond Icon and a new Joule Web Client plugin should load and allow you to interact with Joule as shown below.

Image 11

This completes the Joule integration with your SAP Integrated Business Planning.

If you have any issues, you can reach us via SAP Incidents. Use the following components: “CA-JOULE” for general issues and “BC-CP-MP-FOR” for Joule Booster formation issues.  

Credits and special thanks to Laura Tozzo, Rudiger Meyfarth, Heiko Steffen, Aditi Shrivastava, and the IBP Team. 

Happy Learning!!!

Regards,
Nagesh Caparthy
SAP Business AI RIG Team

If you have any questions about the setup, you can reach our inbox with the Subject line "Joule in SAP IBP questions or issues" at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com.

entry.title: Focused Build SP15: Work packages unassignment from requirements
entry.content:
Introduction

We have released Service Pack(SP) 15 of Focused Build (ST-OST) earlier this month and as all ways with the new SP a lot of exciting and valuable features have been added to our Focused Build fiori applications. You can find more information about all the improvements in different areas of the Requirements-to-deploy process in the blog detailed out here.

In this blog post let's deep dive on the work packages unassignment from requirements via Requirements Management application in detail.

Work packages unassignment from Requirements

The un-assignment of work packages from requirements is an existing feature of Requirements Management app since many SPs. Up to SP09 it was possible to unassign work packages up to status "Handed-over to Release" from requirements. This lead to a deadlock in case the last work package has been removed. So with SP10 we changed to a more restrictive customizing not allowing unassignment of work packages in a later status.

Fig 1. Work packages unassignment from Requirements till SP14

With SP15 release we have further improved this behaviour to avoid such deadlock situation completely. Now it is possible to unassign the work packages from requirements till the status "To be Tested". Also once a work package is unassigned from the requirement the status of the requirement also changes based on the remaining work packages of the same requirement.

Fig 2. Work packages unassignment from Requirements

Before work packages are assigned from requirements in the backend we are now displaying a confirmation message to the user stating the new status of the requirements as a result of the unassignment if the user proceeds. If the user choses cancel button in the confirmation box then the work package unassignment process is cancelled for the user.

Fig 3. Work packages unassignment confirmation message

Please also find the below activity diagram which explains the work packages unassignment process and the requirements status change as a result of unassignment.

Fig 4. Work packages unassignment activity diagram

Key Takeaways

  • With ST-OST 15 now it is possible to unassign work packages from requirements at a much higher status preventing possible deadlock situation.
  • A confirmation message is shown to the user before unassignment and the unassignment proceeds only if user confirms the status change of the associated requirements.

Please feel free to provide feedback either directly here in the comment section or, in case of questions, you can submit them here. You are also encouraged to follow SAP Solution Manager tag  here and Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager tag  here. 

Important Links and Resources

  • Usage Rights
  • SP15 delivered features overview blog
  • Focused Build SP15: Improvements in Mass Change Operations Fiori Application
  • Focused Build SP15: General Improvements in Generic Application
  • Read how SAP Activate and Focused Build with SAP Solution Manager is working together
  • ALM Community page

entry.title: Re: How to do navigation in smarttable from master to det...
entry.content:
Hi,

Sorry for the very late reply. Hope you already came up with a solution.

However, for others out there, should check out the following SAPUI5 sample.

SmartTable with responsive table smart filters will be available as is (CDS View & Metadata based). 

For Navigation (custom) you must add additional Table/Column/Item/ColumnListItem elements and add then use press  event and capture it in your controller. Navigate from there onwards, like show below

<mvc:View
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:smartFilterBar="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"
    xmlns:smartTable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
    xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1"
    controllerName="com.tl.zapcustom.controller.Main"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    core:require="{formatter: 'com/tl/zapcustom/model/formatter'}"
    height="100%"
>
    <Page enableScrolling="true">
        <content>
            <smartFilterBar:SmartFilterBar
                id="smartFilterBar"
                entitySet="Master"
                persistencyKey="SmartFilter_Explored"
            />
            <smartTable:SmartTable
                id="idSmartTable"
                entitySet="Master"
                smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
                tableType="ResponsiveTable"
                enableExport="true"
                beforeExport="onBeforeExport"
                useVariantManagement="true"
                useTablePersonalisation="true"
                header="Approval Documents"
                showRowCount="true"
                persistencyKey="SmartTableAnalytical_Explored"
                enableAutoBinding="true"
                demandPopin="true"
                class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding"
                enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
                editTogglable="true"
                app:useSmartToggle="true"
            >
                <Table
                    alternateRowColors="true"
                    sticky="ColumnHeaders,HeaderToolbar"
                >
                    <!-- Header -->
                    <columns />
                    <!-- Data -->
                    <items>
                        <ColumnListItem
                            id="idColumnListItem"
                            type="Navigation"
                            press="onColumnListItemPress"
                        >
                            <cells />
                        </ColumnListItem>
                    </items>
                </Table>
            </smartTable:SmartTable>
        </content>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

And here is the code for event

onColumnListItemPress: function (oEvent) {
				this.getRouter().navTo("RouteDetails", {
					Docid: oEvent
						.getSource()
						.getBindingContext()
						.getProperty("Docid"),
					Docnumber: oEvent
						.getSource()
						.getBindingContext()
						.getProperty("Docnumber"),
				})
			}

entry.title: Fixing pricing date by customer in Sales Order
entry.content:
Good morning,

I would like to inquire if there is a way to set a fixed pricing date for sales orders.

Specifically, starting from January 1st, 2025, when a price increase will take effect, we would like to apply automatically the old prices for some customers by using a pricing date of December 15th, 2024.

Is there a transaction in SAP where this can be configured?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: S4HC Public -> SAC - Delta / Incremental Transactional Data Loads
entry.content:
Hello,

we would like to implement the Delta / Incremental Transactional Data Loads functionality in SAC based on ACDOCA table in S4HC Public, but even after reading what we could find on the internet, we are not able to make it work. Our settings:

SAC Model: Planning - New Model Type

S4HC Public Architecture: 2 Layer - QAS and PROD

Connection: SAP S/4HANA Acquire Connection

From these two articles (attached below) I have come to a conclussion, that we have to create an OData service from the ACDOCA table and create a OData connection to it in SAC and then it should work. Problem is, that in our 2 layer S4HC Public architecture, there is no DEV layer and therefore it is not possible to develop these kinds of things. In would only work in the 3 layer S4HC Public architecture.

Is my understanding correct or is there some other way that I have not found?

Thank you very much for any help!
Merry Christmas,

Adam

A Simple Guide to Exposing an oData Service from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to the SAP Business Technology Platform Using Developer Extensibility

Solved: Expose CDS View as OData Service/API in S/4HANA Pu... - SAP Community

entry.title: Assign Product Type Group to a Purchasing Info Record
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are looking for a way to add the Product Type Group to a Purchasing Info Record.

Business Case: 

  • Within a Production Order an Operation is created.
  • This Operation uses a Purchasing Info Record. The Purchasing Info Record is not linked to a Material Number.
  • The Operation automatically creates a Purchase Requisition Item.
  • However, since the Purchase Requisition Item does not contain a specific Product Type Group, it is set automatically to 1 (Material).

For all Services this process is faulty, as we need a possibility to set the Product Type Group automatically to 2 (Service)

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP LaMa Repository for UNIX Adaptive Extensions
entry.content:
Hello,

i have a problem setting up a repository for the SAP Adaptive Extensions for UNIX-Server. I have created the repository and placed the latest UNIX SAR file (SAP TECHNOLOGY COMPONENTS/SAP ADAPTIVE EXTENSIONS/SAP ADAPTIVE EXTENSION 1.0 EXT/SAPACEXT_89-20010403.SAR) in the shared directory. However, when I try to create a configuration, it does not recognize the .SAR file. I always get the warning: "Nothing found in repository SAPACEXT_UNIX. Please check if the repository share is configured correctly."

When I place the SAR file for Windows there, it is automatically been recognized but not for UNIX. Does anyone have an idea why the Adaptive Extensions for Linux are not being recognized by LAMA?

 

Kind regards,

Marcel

entry.title: Posting Change Process in RF using the posting change Document number
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have a Scenario where Posting change request to move Products from SLOC A to SLOC B is raised in S4 via MIGO  . Using this Posting change document number need to perform the process using RF . Is there a Logical transaction available in RF as i don't see any menu available in standard

Process Steps:

1. Perform MIGO with Mvt Type 311 with material , Product, Plant , source Sloc, Dest. Sloc

2. Delivery Document created in S4 .

3. Same document replicated to ewm as Posting change document.

Now using RF i want to perform the process of Creation of warehouse Task and confirmation with reference to this posting change Document .Is this possible in standard RF or do we need custom RF transaction to be created.

Thanks

Arjun

entry.title: AIWarmup - Transformers and "Attention is All You Need" - A first dive
entry.content:
Credits

  • Credit for this AI Warmup goes to the excellent material - both code and videos - from:
    • Ajay-Halthor CodeEmporium
    • Sasha Rush The annotated transformer and
    • Andrej Karpathy Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
  • These, and more are shared in our "Attention is all you need" Crowdsourced Upskilling Path
  • This AI Warmup is on github here. More on Commerce and Consumer Industries Garage Upskilling Programs here

Video

A video of me walking through this journey is here: I suggest watching that while going through this journey/blog. 🙂

Contents

  • Introducing Transformers
  • LLMs - Level 1
  • LLMs - Level 2
  • LLMs - Level 3
  • LLMs - Level 4
  • LLMs - Ground Level

Caveats:

  • In this warmup, we assume that a "word" is the central building block for LLMs, and that our training units are single sentences.
  • we use terms human-like terms "thought", "meaning", "learning", but all is ultimately just number crunching

LLM in one view:

Introducing Transformers

  • The current Transformer "goldrush started with the seminal paper: Attention is all you need.
  • This turbo charged LLMs..
    • allowing much faster training: as an LLM could train on a whole sentence at once, rather than word by word which is much slower
    • allowing better linguistic understanding: as the LLM could view over an entire sentence at once, allowing all words to be aware and influenced by each other, and not just linearly along a sentence.
  • This was made possible by a technique called "Attention" (Not new in itself, but this was a first attempt to use ONLY attention for LLM trainings)
  • In this AI Warmup I try to navigate a middle path between hand-waving general descriptions, and hardcore coding of the Transformer.
  • To dive deep into the code of a Transformer, I recommend you:
    • read Daniel Dugas' high level description, then
    • go through Ajay's video, then
    • study the code behind the the colab here, and then
    • watch Andrej Karpathy's series Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
    • and/or join a future AI Warmup from me 🙂

You can code, train and run a Transformer yourself straight away, online:

  • This colab is a coded version of the "Attention is all you need" paper, running on a Jupyter Notebook on Google infrastructure.
  • It is based entirely on Sasha's site "The annotated Transformer", with just a few tweeks from me to make it work (again).
  • Simply open it and follow the steps..

LLMs - Level 1

  • At the heart of an LLM, such as Chat GPT, is the Transformer.
  • At the heart of the Transformer are two concepts:
    • 1: a "Transformer Space" and
    • 2: the "Attention Mechanism"

1: Transformer Space

  • Perhaps "Linguistice Space" would be a better name?
  • Consider an example 2-dimensional space, whose 2 axis help us position some words in it, in a meaningful way.
  • We can imagine that if our space has many more dimensions
    • we could assign other meanings to each axis
    • and that would allow us to place words quite "well/accurately/meaningfully" (examples from here)
  • Consider Twenty questions
    • where you are expected to guess a word, after asking at most 20 "yes/no questions"
    • "Is it a vegetable", "Can you eat it", "Is it green" -> is it an apple? -> "Yes! Party and gloat at your own brilliance."
    • which is somewhat equivalent to placing words in a 20 dimensional space, to guess what it is
  • The location of a word in the space is called a word's embedding and can be described with coordinates/vector
    • See for example https://projector.tensorflow.org/?config=https://ecraft2learn.github.io/ai/word-embeddings/de/projector.json
  • You can think of the multi dimensional space (axis, locations) as being Language Agnostic :
    • an English/German/French/Swahili space would all have a similar structure
    • in a sense the space is encoding something "higher" than actual pronounced words, but rather their meanings

Transformer Space on Steroids

  • The Transformer Space is a high-dimension space. Depending on precise implementation..
    • GPT-2 uses 1024 dimensional space
    • GPT-3 uses 12288 dimensional space
    • GPT-4 uses > 12k dimensional space
  • With so many dimensions, the LLM is able to "place/embed" not just words, but "contect-rich words" and ultimately "meaning"/"thought".
  • Initially this space is populated with "plain", "context-free" words.
    • Picture all the words from the dictionary being placed in "sensible" locations in this multi-dimensional space
    • Their positions likely move during training
  • Interestingly, it turns out that a Transformer Space of N-dimensions, can actually represent an exponentially higher number of (almost) independent axis (See this video). Thus any one location in this space can have 100000s of independent meanings embedded in it)

2: Attention Mechanism

  • Placing plain words in the space is "easy"
  • But we know that the meaning of a word, is affected by other words in the sentence.
  • This influence between words is called "Attention", and during training the LLM creates an effective Attention Mechanism
  • We will consider the sentence "My fluffy white cute cat statue." later on, when examining Attention
  • For now consider that the LLM needs to learn to associate words with each other correctly..

Embeddings of Thoughts and Meanings..

  • We start with just a "dictionary" or words in the space
  • During training, the LLM will learn to derive and embed many many more points into this space, that represent "highly nuanced words" / "context-rich words" / "meanings" / "thoughts"
  • At the end of the training, a remarkable property is possible..

Asking a trained LLM "What should the next word be?"

  • Think of these new "context-rich" points in the space, as enabling the LLM to answer the question "If I am at this point in the space, what should the next word be?"..
  • When we give the beginning of a sentence to a trained LLM, the LLM uses the sentence prefix to guide it around this pre-trained space.
  • Once its journey is complete, it has arrived somewhere in that space, and that location/vector in the space ALONE is used to determine what the next word should be
  • To restress this fundamental, and remarkable point:
  • When a trained LLM is given a (short/long) sentence "Once upon a time, ....., and the detective concluded that the murderer was ...", the LLM will use this text to jump around and eventually land at a point in this space, and that single point ALONE is used to determine the next word to output, such as "Teddy"
  • This is remarkable and true..

(Source https://youtu.be/LPZh9BOjkQs?si=RIkSdkTvE2zmiVjT)

Training goal

So for this Level 1 discussion, the purpose of training a Tranformer is - to populate its space with context-rich, language agnostic meaning - to create a well tuned, language agnostic attention mechanism

LLMs - Level 2

We keep using phrases like "well tuned", "trained", "learns how to..."

So we need to talk about Neural Networks: the part of LLMs that do the learning

Neural Networks

When to use NNs?

  • NNs are good at "solving" a particular type of problem
  • If you can shape your problem such that..
    • its behaviour is consistent
    • you have a lot of "correct" input-output examples,
    • these examples can be encoded as a sequence of numbers as input, and a sequence of numbers as output
    • you can "grade" the proposed output numerically, with a simple maths equation
  • then NNs can help you

Consider the image recogniser, that learns how to map a 2D image to a 1D list.

Why is this of interest?

Picture an LLM from outside: - Input=Sentence prefix in -> LLM -> Output=Proposed word(s) - This can be shaped in to an NN type of problem..

NNs and Mappings..

  • NNs ultimately map from one sequence to another sequence shape

How they learn..

  • While training a NN:
    • we apply an example input
    • we compare its output with the "correct" output
    • we adjust the weights a little to improve the answer (using Back propogation)
    • we repeat millions of times..

(https://youtu.be/LPZh9BOjkQs?si=ipE7Uf_N27m-NZUF&t=215)

In our LLM we have several locations where NNs will help us:

  • mapping from initial words to good locations in our space
  • mapping from our space to a (graded) list of words
  • mapping a list to and from a longer list

Note the example digit recogniser has ~120,000 weights/parameters. For comparison, the NNs in a GPT have more... - GPT-3.5 has ~175 billion parameters - GPT-4 has ~1.5 trillion parameters

With 1 Billion computations a second, a computer would take over 100 Million Years to train the current large LLMs.

Training goal in more detail..

The purpose of training a Transformer is: - to assign helpful "meanings/ linguistic differentiators" to each axis - to populate its space with context-rich points - to create a well tuned and helpful attention mechanism - to train the NNs to perform their mapping well - such that, the LLM is able to predict the next word very well.

Training strategy

  • We have a lot of training data - petabyes of sentences from the internet, wikipedia etc.
  • Consider the sentence "My name is Bob the Builder and I drive trains."
    • This sentence has (at least) ELEVEN things an LLM can learn:
      • "My" is likely to start a sentence
      • "name" is likely to follow "My"
      • "is" is likely to follow "My name"
      • "Bob" is likely to follow "My name is"
      • ..
      • "." is likely to follow "My name is Bob the Builder and I drive trains"
  • Transformers use a neat approach, called ("Masked Attention"), that
    • shapes the 11 lessons into input-output pairs
    • allows the LLM to learn all eleven things at once
  • The LLM will also learn the indirect lessons (via "Attention"), such as
    • if "Bob" and "train" appear in a sentence, "Builder" is likely too appear
    • "Bob the Builder" is a train driver ..
  • Note too, the relative positions of each word in the sentence is important and something the NNs should be aware of. So this too is encoded for each word ("Positonal Encoding")

Upon Training Completion..

  • After training..
    • the "meanings" of each axis of the MDS are fixed
    • the MDS is full of well positioned "plain words", but
    • more importantly full of well positioned context-rich meaning"
    • the Attention mechanism has been well tuned
    • the NNs have been well tuned to do their required jobs well

Attention

Intro..

  • Remember that our highly-dimensional space contains "plain words" but also "context-rich words"/"meanings"/"thoughts"
  • The purpose of "Attention" is to take all the plain words plucked from an input sentence, and move them to locations that better represent their "context-rich word"/"meaning"/"thought".
  • Consider the sentence: "My fluffy white cute cat statue"
    • the precise ("context-rich") meaning of each word within this sentence is shaped by other words in the sentence (and beyond, but lets ignore that for now)
    • for example
      • the location of our generic/plain "cat" in our highly dimensional space, should be moved by the words before it - to represent more precisely that it is a "big white cute cat"
      • we can also sense that the word "statue" is very important in this sentence - it should have more effect on placing the cat, than the other words.
      • also the idea that is it my cat should somehow be encoded in this word cat (moving it along a me/your axis perhaps)

A strategy for Attention: "QKV"

  • For each word in the sentence:
    • map the word to its "plain word" location in the space
    • look at words around this word and learn (via training) what types of words should move this word, and by how much
  • A bit more flesh.. Let us consider one use case: how words might influence nouns...
    • Take the initial sentence as input and get the "plain" embeddings, then...
      • each word should be able to "Query the other words, with "are you a noun-influencer?"
      • each word should have a "Key" describing how important a noun-influencer it is (0=not at all, 0.1=a bit, 1.0=very much ("Statue"))
      • each word should have a "Value" that describes what it thinks its own influence should be on the word that asked (i.e. how the querying word should be moved)..
      • sum up the values, weighted by their respective Key, and move the word accordingly...

The Attention Strategy: A Single Head of Attention

  • We create a NN that is powerful enough to implement that QKV strategy
    • each word in its input should be able to "Query the other words, with "are you an xyz"
    • each word should have a "Key" describing how important an xyz it is (0=not an xyz at all, 0.1= an unimportant xyz, 1.0=an important xyz)
    • each word should have a "Value" that describes what it thinks its influence should be (how the querying word should be moved)..
    • take all the values and their respective keys, sum them appropriately and move the word...
  • The NN/LLMs themselves figure out what the xyz itself should be, and what the Q,K,Vs are of every word should be
  • This strategy is called a "Head of Attention"

Multi Headed Attention

  • The LLMs run many of these independently in parallel (GPT3 has 96 of them(?)), and this is called "Multi Headed Attention"
  • This allows the LLM to develop attention not just around adjectives, but other grammatical/semantic properties: perhaps verbs, tense, posession,....
  • The NNs figure this out, using the goal of improving the LLMs performance

High level architecture

The Transformer architecture from the original paper, is for translating one language to another. Chat GPT, and text generators use just part of this..

The left side: a full transformer

The right side: a text generator

Note Nx = 96 for GPT 3

LLMs - Level 3

  • Tensors

It's all just Numbers..

  • Our transformer is a big number cruncher - numbers in, numbers out
  • The architecture of a transformer consists of a pipeline with a mix of 2 types of number crunchers,
    • Fixed Mathematical Operations - massaging, shaping, scaling numbers
    • Learnable Mathematical Operations - NNs with learnable parameters

Number crunching is done with Tensors

  • Pytorch is our number crunching library, and uses Tensors
    • a Tensor can have different shapes, and contains just numbers
    • a "1D Tensor" with shape 1xD..
      • is a sequence ("an array"/"a vector") of D numbers.
      • A word's embedding is represented by this sort of Tensor (a point/coordinate in the D-dimensional space)
      • [ 1.0, 1.1, -2.1, 1.23423452, ... ]
    • a "2D Tensor" with shape TxD..
      • is a matrix of T by D numbers
      • A sentence is represented by this sort of Tensor
      • [ [ 1.0, 1.1, -2.1, 1.234234523 ], [ 1.0, 1.1, -2.1, 1.234234523 ] ]
    • a "3D Tensor" with shape BxTxD..
      • is a sequence of matrices,
      • A set of B sentences is represented by this sort of Tensor
    • a Tensor of any number of dimensions is possible: 4,5,6...
    • pytorch/python can process tensors on GPUs very very fast, with massive parallelisation

See Pytorch Documentation: https://pytorch.org/tutorials

Revisiting the 2 types of number crunchers:

  • Our two types of number crunchers are:
    • Fixed mathematical operations - massaging, multiplying, reshaping Tensors (Tensors in -> Crunch -> Tensors out)
    • NNs: Tensor in -> NN of varying shapes (with Learnable Parameters) -> Tensor out

The NNs do the "learning" necessary to make the transfomer do better and better during training: The fixed maths/torch operations, do the numerical heavy lifting

But we need a Reality check: for a simple NN, for example to recognise hand-written numbers ..

  • it helps to reason/imagine what each perceptron is "trying to do" ("anthropomorphic claims..")
  • but what each perceptron is actually doing/representing is much harder to describe/understand/abstract
  • what matters is that we provide the NN with enough perceptrons and enough layers, to give it enough "freedom"/"dimensions" to be able to learn to do what we want it to do ..
  • the actual internal behaviour is hard to parse (just like the actual behaviour of individual neurons within our brain)

LLMs - Level 4

  • In a high-level overview of a Transformer, we expect to see a flow from input to output, with:
    • NNs with their learnable parameters
    • fixed math/torch functions that prepare/massage/reshape/process the tensors between the NNs

Our toolkit has:

  • 1D Tensors:
  • 2D Tensors:
  • 3D Tensors:
  • Attention with QKV Tensors
  • NNs with their learnable parameters:

Architecture Overviews

You can view these full-scale images in the attachments.

LLMs - Ground Level

  • To really dive deep into the Transformer python code, I recommend you:
    • read Daniel Dugas' high level description, then
    • go through Ajay's video, then
    • study the code behind the the colab here, and then
    • watch Andrej Karpathy's series Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
    • and/or join a future AI Warmup from me 🙂

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Enablement: Live Product Expert Sessions in 2025
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

We at Cloud ERP Product Success Enablement and Adoption strive to enable our valued customers and partners to take advantage of our fully-fledged SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution. Therefore, we offer various enablement assets, from product experts to product experts, explaining the features and functions as well as the benefits of innovations.

In the following, I’d like to shed some light on our various live product expert sessions. As of today, the team offers three different types of live sessions:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Early Release Series
  • In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training


This table helps you to get an overview of the available sessions as well as the content that is covered during the different formats. If you’re interested in more detail and would like to follow regular updates, check out the individual landing pages on learning.sap.com linked below.

 

Live Product Expert SessionContent CoveredFrequencyTarget AudienceAccess Requirements
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Early Release SeriesTalk about and demo key innovations for upcoming major SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition releases

Bi-yearly

Customers, partners, SAP InternalsActive SAP Learning Hub License needed*
In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionDeep-dive knowledge transfer sessions independently from releases.Monthly based on demandCustomers, partners, SAP InternalsActive SAP Learning Hub License needed*
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training~ 7 days of training covering broad and deep-dive product knowledge for all SAP S/4HANA Cloud releases.

Bi-yearly

Partners and SAP internals

Active SAP Learning Hub License needed**

*Get access to Support Edition for Customers & Partners // Internal Edition for SAP Employees.
**Access Partner Edge here or send a mail to partner@sap.com if you face any issues.

 

Topics Covered

We run these live product expert sessions along many lines of business and industries as well as other cross topics such as:

  • Artificial Intelligence 
  • SAP User Experience
  • Joule
  • Finance
  • GRC
  • Manufacturing and Discrete Industries
  • Supply Chain
  • Sales
  • Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management
  • Professional Services
  • Sustainability
  • Product Compliance
  • Environment, Health, and Safety
  • Service
  • Sourcing & Procurement
  • Asset Management
  • Solution Order Management
  • Consumer Products
  • Retail
  • Master Data Management
  • Technology Topics
  • SAP Activate
  • SAP Cloud ALM
  • Test Automation Tool 
  • Two-Tier ERP
  • SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC)

And more!

Live Product Expert Sessions Throughout 2025

Fig. 1: Overview of Live Product Expert Sessions planned throughout 2025. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release and update cycle is following SAP’s Release Plan and may be changed by SAP without notice, therefore the global enablement plans can change as well.  

With the above focus of the sessions in mind, let’s take a look at the global live session plan for 2025. For the two major releases SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508, we plan on showing and demoing highlights of the releases during the Early Release Series which is happening 1-2 weeks before shipment.

In March, we plan to host the first Product Expert Training of this year with a focus on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. In the second half of the year (H2) 2025, we have planned for the next Product Expert Training focusing on SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2508.

In an almost monthly cadence, based on demand, we’re hosting In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition which is a format that is running independently from releases. In these sessions, we cover specific topics in-depth to get a more holistic insight into certain aspects of the product. As this series is not bound to releases, it depends on topic suggestions from our stakeholders. If you’d like to nominate topics, simply do so via this blog post.

 

How to Sign Up for the SAP Enterprise Support Edition as a Customer/Partner

In order to watch the recordings, you will need to have an active SAP Learning Hub license with at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition. Find out how to register for the SAP Enterprise Support edition in case you don’t already have access to any of the editions yet:

  • Navigate to the sign up page 
  • Click the Sign up button. Authenticate yourself with your s-user ID and password. Upon first access, the system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalog respectively. Within two hours, you will then receive a registration confirmation via e-mail.
  • After you have received the confirmation e-mail, the registration process is complete.

Partners can access the recordings through an SAP Partner Edge Edition.

 

How to Sign Up for the Internal Edition as an SAP Employee

In order to watch the recordings, you will need to have an SAP Learning Hub license. In case you have no edition assigned to your profile, please register here for the Internal Edition which is free of charge.

Make sure to be logged onto the SAP Learning Hub before opening the URL. In case the link doesn't open, copy and paste it into your browser. If you face any other issues with your login, please contact the help center.

Follow the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tag and the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with the latest blog posts. 

 

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News December 2024
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve with new features that enhance project management, monitoring, and traceability. Here are the top 10 highlights from the latest December 2024 update:

1. Document Service Management (DMS) Integration

Using SAP Document Management service, users can upload, replace, download, and delete external files (PDFs, PPTs, etc.) directly in SAP Cloud ALM. This feature involves establishing a connection with SAP Document Management service and includes UI notifications for connection issues.

 

2. Relations between Configuration Elements and Process Hierarchy Nodes

Users can document how configurations relate to the overall process hierarchy by creating and displaying relations between configuration elements and process hierarchy nodes.

3. Assign Project Tasks to Configuration Elements

Project tasks can now be assigned to configuration elements in the Tasks app and Libraries app to track and perform necessary tasks for the configuration elements.

4. Relations to Application Libraries Available

Users can assign or unassign relations to application libraries from within the Documents app, making it easier to manage document relations.

5. Check for Transport Readiness When Approving Features for Production

A feature's status change to "Ready for Production" now includes an automatic check for the readiness of associated transports.

6. Transport Checks

Perform Downgrade Protection and Cross-Reference Checks on CTS-managed transports to evaluate the impact on production environments. Users can perform these checks individually or together.

7. Update Predecessors in Spreadsheet

The “Predecessor Row” column can now be updated in spreadsheet uploads for existing tasks.

8. View Relations to Issues and Actions

Task detail views now display related issues and actions for follow-up tasks created from these sources.

9. Business Objects Page

A new page for visualizing business objects in your landscape is available. It includes monitoring of master data replication processes with detailed drill-down capabilities.

 10. Monitoring Enhancements

  • SAP Ariba Shopping - Mobile: Detailed status monitoring of incoming and outgoing messages.
  • SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management: Detailed status monitoring of messages.
  • SAP Market Communication for Utilities: Detailed status monitoring of messages.
  • Monitoring for SAP Digital Manufacturing Service:Job execution data for SAP Digital Manufacturing service can now be monitored using the Job & Automation Monitoring app.

10: Simplified collection method for SAP Master Data Integration

The data collection for SAP Master Data Integration service is now orchestrated using Next Generation – Data Collection Infrastructure (NG-DCI). This simplifies the configuration process and allows for the automatic setup of services with SAP Cloud ALM.

These top 10 features in the December 2024 SAP Cloud ALM update are designed to streamline operations, improve management efficiency, and enhance user experience. For more detailed information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

Stay tuned for more exciting updates as SAP continues to innovate and improve the #Cloud ALM experience!

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to CAP CDS 8: A living document of fixes and lessons learned
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for sharing your findings!

> The new Protocol Adapters (GA) causes logic errors when sending a create payload containing an association. They should only be creatable when it is a composition.

Also the old protocol adapter didn't allow creating instances via associations. It was only possible to set the foreign keys, all other properties were silently ignored. Example:

PATCH /Orders(123)

{ "myAssoc": { ID: 1, nonKey: 'this value will be filtered out' } }

This will only work if the association target already exists. It's not allowed to modify instances along associations. The life cycle is separate.

entry.title: A journey from Netweaver 7.50 to S/4HANA - Part 1: Setting the Stage
entry.content:
This is the beginning of a multi-part blog post series and right now, I can neither tell you, how many episodes it will eventually have, how much time will elapse between the episodes and for how long the series will run all told. Your guess is basically as good as mine!

Part 1 will mostly set the stage and briefly explain our migration project to move from a NetWeaver 7.50 EHP8 system landscape to S/4HANA 2309 (hosted in the cloud but "on premise") with the plan to be completely done in 2027 before NW7.50 goes out of maintenance.

Our current system landscape

We have two main ERP landscapes, one for worldwide (WW) systems which cater to the needs of our subsidiaries around the globe and another one for the headquarter (HQ). Both landscapes are basically 3-tier with Dev - Test - Prod. However, the WW landscape has 4 test and 4 prod systems roughly aligned by different timezones (Europe, Americas, Pacific and Middle East) but they share the same code base.

Simplified system landscape

In addition, we have one central development system which is mostly used to implement OSS-notes which then feed into both landscapes to keep them in sync. This system is also used for the maintenance of dictionary objects in our Z-namespace.

We have been on a HANA database for several years already and have a central ATC-system available which has already been set up as an S/4HANA system, so we can run all the needed S/4HANA readiness checks from there.

The conversion to Business Partner hasn't happened yet but is getting underway right now.

Our rough plan for the migration

A few weeks ago we had the official kick-off for our transform project which will be a joint project done by IT and user departments and split into several teams. Given that I'm part of the developers team, this series will focus on topics related to - guess what? - ABAP development activites!

This is how we plan to do the project:

We will do the migration and conversion for the (large) worldwide landscape first, but in steps from small to large systems and not with a big bang at the end. As a first step, the central (#1 in the image above) and WW development (#2) systems will be copied to new dev systems which will then go through all the necessary conversion steps to get them to S/4HANA, leveraging information provided by @OlgaDolinskaja (and others) in presentations like this one about the Custom Code Migration App. We already know that this will be quite a big task given the thousands of findings the S/4HANA readiness checks found. We do have some hope, though, that a good chunk of them will be possible to get under control with the help of quick fixes.

On the other hand, we have pretty good usage data collected for almost 5 years which we can import into the development systems in order to eliminate no longer used custom code with the help of the Custom Code Migration app before we even look at the readiness check findings in detail. If we are lucky, this will take about 1/3 (if not more) of the objects out of the equation as we'll move them to a deletion transport and get rid of them for good.

In parallel, the smallest prod system will be copied (Middle East, #3) and converted via the transports from the new development system so that testing can start as soon as possible in a "test project system". This step will also include the switch to business partner and will therefore make cleaning up of the master data necessary

Once this first system is "up and running" properly, it'll be rinse and repeat for the next WW system and the first system will lay dormant for quite some time as we move to the next one. If everything goes according to plan, about a year later, the four WW-systems will be ready for the switch to S/4HANA sometime in 2026 with the go-lives staged a few weeks apart after a most likely lengthy and in-depth testing phase.

Afterwards, we'll go through the excercise again for the headquarter systems which we want to have the go-live for in 2027.

Some other considerations

Not too surprisingly, we'll also have to deal with many add-ons, some of which have been implemented in the Z-namespace. There are also large country specific developments like the ones for Nota Fiscal in Brazil or the tax handling in the USA which will require involvement from developers tackling these objects locally in some cases with the help of external consultants. And, there will be surprises lurking for us as we move along!

And before I forget: There’ll obviously be concurrent changes in the “legacy” ERP NW7.50 landscape for which we’ll set up some kind of retrofit to incorporate changes made in the code base adapted for S/4HANA. We just hope that these changes will not come up with too many S/4HANA Readiness Check findings! One can always hope for the best, right?

So, that's the admittedly very rough sketch of what we'll be busy with for the next 2 1/2 years. I'd be very interested in any thoughts and feedback any of you might have - especially if you have gone through a comparable project already and know where some large and small stumbling blocks might be we should be aware of.

Thanks for reading and hope you'll join me for this journey!

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Transaction Management in SAP Commerce Cloud: Isolation, Integrity, and Use Case Examples
entry.content:
What are transactions? Transactions are a series of database operations that must be executed entirely or not at all to maintain data integrity. If any operation within the transaction fails, the entire transaction is rolled back, and changes are undone, ensuring database consistency.

Transaction isolation levels in SAP Commerce Different transaction isolation levels control how transactions affect each other in database systems:

TRANSACTION_NONE – No isolation, transactions can affect each other, which may lead to undesirable results.

TRANSACTION_READ_UNCOMMITTED – Allows "dirty reads," which can result in incorrect data.

TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED – Prevents dirty reads but still allows "non-repeatable reads."

TRANSACTION_REPEATABLE_READ – Prevents dirty and non-repeatable reads but "phantom reads" may still occur.

TRANSACTION_SERIALIZABLE – The highest level of isolation, ensuring full data integrity, but at the cost of performance.

The default isolation level in SAP Commerce is TRANSACTION_READ_UNCOMMITTED.

 

 

public class TestUserService {

    private ModelService modelService;
    private UserService userService;

    public void registerNewUserTransactionBody(String userEmail, String userPassword) throws Exception {
        Transaction.current().execute(new TransactionBody() {

            @Override
            public Object execute() {
                if (userService.isUserExisting(userEmail)) {
                    throw new IllegalArgumentException("User with this email address already exists!");
                }

                CustomerModel newUser = createNewUser(userEmail, userPassword);
                modelService.save(newUser);

                return null;
            }

        });
    }

    public void registerNewUser(String userEmail, String userPassword) {
        Transaction tx = Transaction.current();
        tx.setTransactionIsolationLevel(Connection.TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED);

        tx.begin();
        boolean success = false;

        try {
            if (userService.isUserExisting(userEmail)) {
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("User with this email address already exists!");
            }

            CustomerModel newUser = createNewUser(userEmail, userPassword);
            modelService.save(newUser);
            success = true;

        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
        } finally {
            if (success) {
                tx.commit();
            } else {
                tx.rollback();
            }
        }
    }

    private CustomerModel createNewUser(String userEmail, String userPassword) {
        CustomerModel user = new CustomerModel();
        user.setUid(userEmail);
        user.setPassword(userPassword);
        return user;
    }

    public void setModelService(ModelService modelService) {
        this.modelService = modelService;
    }

    public void setUserService(UserService userService) {
        this.userService = userService;
    }
}

 

 

Transaction Elements Explanation:

  1. Transaction.current().execute(new TransactionBody() {...}):

    • The transaction is executed within the execute() method, ensuring that all operations (e.g., checking if the user exists, creating a new user, saving the user) are part of one transaction. If an error occurs, the transaction is automatically rolled back.

  2. Transaction tx = Transaction.current();:

    • Creates a transaction object representing the current transaction.

  3. tx.setTransactionIsolationLevel(Connection.TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED);:

    • Sets the isolation level to TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED, which prevents reading uncommitted data (dirty reads).

  4. tx.begin();:

    • Starts the transaction.

  5. tx.commit();:

    • Commits the transaction, saving the changes to the database.

  6. tx.rollback();:

    • Rolls back the transaction, discarding any changes made during the transaction if an error occurs.

Summary:

  • First method (TransactionBody): The transaction is executed inside the execute() method within a TransactionBody object. This is a more concise way of handling transactions since you don't have to manually begin or commit the transaction. All operations within the transaction are encapsulated in one unit.
  • Second method (manual transaction management): In this approach, the transaction is manually managed — you start it with tx.begin(), and then commit (tx.commit()) or roll back (tx.rollback()) it based on the success of the operation. Additionally, the transaction isolation level (e.g., TRANSACTION_READ_COMMITTED) can be controlled. This is a more flexible approach, giving full control over the transaction lifecycle.

Both approaches ensure that changes are made atomically (either all or none), which guarantees data integrity in the system.

entry.title: Optional surveys and corresponding notifications trigger behavior before and after system downtime
entry.content:
Dear team,

Soon we will have a SAP SF system downtime during more than 1 month, due to EC implementation, where our LMS system will be also affected.

Therefore, we have some questions we would like to clarify regarding what we can expected from system behavior during and after this downtime:

  • For optional surveys, currently they are not visible in the new ILX (new Learning Experience) and only in the legacy, but they are planned to be part of H1 2025 release - in case we have users completing trainings meantime, but before the release period - will optional surveys being triggered for those users, even not displayed? Or they will only start being triggered and displayed after the H1 2025 release?
  • And about already existing optional surveys displayed on users" learning page on legacy version - will they appear automatically in the new Learning homepage after the H1 2025 release?
  • For training effectiveness surveys, that are being triggered for managers 90 days after direct reports" training completion - e.g. training completion by Jan 1, 2025 --> effectiveness survey triggered April 1, 2025), since our APM jobs will be disabled during the referred system downtime:
    • Once system is back again and corresponding APM  job is enabled, will these training effectiveness survey notifications still being triggered to the managers? Is this 90 days period still counting meantime or is it stopping? If stopping, will it be reactivated after the downtime or is it completely stopped?
    • If notifications will be sent, will they be all triggered once? Is the system able to handle a high volume of notifications?
    • If notifications will be sent, which will be the manager took in account to assign the survey, in case of manager change meantime - the manager on the training completion day (1st January) or the manager on the survey assignment date (1st April) 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Unlock Insights with Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Managing a subscription business often requires a balance between efficient operations and data-driven decision-making. With the introduction of Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics in SAP Subscription Billing, accessing actionable insights has become more seamless than ever.

Follow this guide to uncover the full potential of Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics. These tools empower users to make informed decisions, enhance customer satisfaction, and refine their revenue strategies—all in one place. Let’s dive into the highlights.

Key Features and Value

Comprehensive Metrics and Insights

  •  KPIs at a Glance: Gain instant access to key performance indicators (KPIs) such as active Subscriptions, not Started Subscriptions, expired Subscriptions, Cancelled Subscriptions and Withdrawn Subscriptions. These metrics provide a real-time snapshot of your subscription business's health.

Subscription KPIs

  • Active Subscriptions KPI: Displays the total number of active subscriptions as of today, regardless of the date range filter.
  • Not Started Subscriptions KPI: Reflects the number of subscriptions that have not yet started or are scheduled to begin in the future.
  • New Subscriptions KPI: Shows the count of subscriptions that began within the selected date range, based on applied filters.
  • Expired Subscriptions KPI: Indicates the total number of subscriptions that have expired.
  • Canceled Subscriptions KPI: Reports the number of subscriptions that have been canceled.
  • Withdrawn Subscriptions KPI: Represents the total count of subscriptions that were withdrawn.

Billing Data KPIs

  • Total Billing Amount: Displays the overall billed amount for the selected date range, considering applied filters.
  • Open Billing Amount: Reflects the total outstanding billing amount that has not yet been paid.
  • Number of Customers: Indicates the total number of customers within the selected date range.
  • Average Billing Amount per Customer: Calculates the average billed amount per customer based on the total billing amount and the number of customers.
  • Number of Subscriptions: Shows the total count of subscriptions within the selected date range, considering filters.
  • Average Billing Amount per Subscription: Represents the average billed amount per subscription, derived from the total billing amount and the number of subscriptions.

 

  • Trend Visualizations: Gain insights into billing trends with clear graphical representations over time. This includes billing totals, growth rates, and detailed breakdowns for charges vs. credits, top products, and top markets. Easily identify patterns, such as peaks, declines, and growth opportunities, across different periods and categories. 

  • Advanced Filtering: Quickly segment data by market, product, or customer for targeted analysis. Identify which markets or products are performing well and where there’s room for improvement.

  • Interactive Filtering: You can also click on a bar in the charts to apply filters. For example, if you click on a market ID in the diagram, all affected KPIs and charts will update to display only the relevant data for the selected market.

  • Sorting: It is also possible to sort specific attributes in a ascending or descending order.

Use Cases

  • Market Performance: Understand which products sell best in specific markets and identify underperforming ones.

  • Churn Analysis: Analyze reasons for subscription cancellations. For example, identifying that customers in a particular region cite pricing as a common cancellation reason can help you adjust strategies to reduce churn.

  • Customer Retention: Track customer behaviors and adapt pricing models to improve satisfaction and retention rates.

Getting Started

Activating Data Synchronization

To enable Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics, follow these steps:

  1. Access Business Configuration: Navigate to the Business Configuration section in your SAP Subscription Billing environment.
  2. Open Integration Settings: Locate the “Integration Settings” tab.
  3. Activate Data Synchronization: Enable the synchronization process by clicking the corresponding button in the “Analytics” section.

 

 

  1. Define Currency Exchange Rates: For integrated scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, activate currency exchange rates directly. Otherwise, upload exchange rates via API.
  2. Assign Roles: Ensure that the required roles (“ui_analytics_subscription_view” and “ui_analytics_bill_view”) are assigned to relevant users.

Note: The synchronization process may take up to 24 hours to complete.

Summary

Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics in SAP Subscription Billing provide an all-in-one solution for subscription-based businesses. By leveraging these tools, you can make data-driven decisions, optimize operations, and achieve sustainable growth. Start unlocking your subscription business’s potential today!

entry.title: SAP Fiori Tools 2501 Update: Elevating Development with AI, Usability, and Flexibility
entry.content:
SAP Fiori tools continues to evolve, delivering innovative capabilities that empower developers and casual users alike. The latest enhancements focus on leveraging AI, simplifying the user interface for adaptations, and improving the overall usability of the tools. Let’s explore these new features and what they mean for your SAP Fiori development experience.

Accelerating Projects with AI: Introducing the Project Accelerator

One of the most significant innovations in this release is the Project Accelerator. Designed to simplify the development journey, the Project Accelerator transforms your business requirements—whether expressed in text, images, or a combination of both—into a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project with a fully-functional SAP Fiori elements application. This innovation is inspired by feedback from customers who sought more versatile ways to communicate their project needs.

At the heart of this innovation lies a specialized panel within SAP Fiori tools, purpose-built to harness the power of generative AI. This panel provides an intuitive interface for developers, ensuring that even complex business requirements are effortlessly translated into technical artifacts. The panel can also be accessed from the Joule command “/fiori-gen-spec-app". To use this feature, ensure you have an SAP Build Code subscription, which provides access to the necessary tools.

Upload a markdown file in the project Accelerator with images and text describing the desired application.

Making UI Adaptation More Accessible

For casual users and developers who need to adapt UIs, the Adaptation Editor now offers new capabilities aimed at simplifying the process. Identifying the right controls or understanding the flow of activities to meet their business requirements can often be challenging, especially for beginners. To address this, the Adaptation Editor introduces quick actions for common tasks like adding custom columns or actions to a table. These enhancements are designed to make the editor not only beginner-friendly but also a powerful tool for experienced developers.

List report related quick actions are shown in the upper part of the property panel to the right.

Moreover, the Adaptation Editor ensures a seamless experience by respecting the SAPUI5 version and the type of project, displaying only the actions that are supported. For a detailed breakdown of quick actions and their availability, refer to the Quick Actions Availability Matrix.

Continuous Usability Improvements

SAP Fiori tools has also introduced a series of usability improvements that make development more efficient:

  • Enhanced Page Editor Capabilities: Developers can now add SAP Fiori elements building blocks directly within the Page Editor. Previously limited to Guided Development guides, this functionality streamlines the usage of the flexible programming model.
  • Integrated Workflow with UI Service Generator: After generating services using the UI Service Generator, developers are now prompted to launch the Fiori generator to create an application. Additionally, the UI Service Generator can now be started from ABAP CDS views displayed in the Service Center of SAP Business Application Studio, further enhancing its integration.
  • Adaptation Editor Enhancements: For OData V4-based adaptation projects, the Adaptation Editor now includes manifest settings in the properties pane. This improvement offers greater visibility and control over project configurations.
  • Dynamic Measures in Charts: Guided Development’s chart-related guides now allow developers to create and edit dynamic measures, offering greater flexibility in data visualization.
  • Streamlined Node Module Management: The node module @sap/ux-specification which determines the schema and configuration of applications, is now managed centrally for all new projects. By removing the need to add this as a dev dependency, the process is more resource-efficient and reduces maintenance overhead.

Looking Ahead

SAP Fiori tools continues to innovate, and there’s much more to anticipate, including:

  • AI-Powered Project Updates: Seamlessly update existing projects using an AI-driven conversational interface, streamlining ongoing development.
  • Expanded Quick Actions: Look forward to additional quick actions in the Adaptation Editor and features accessible via the outline context menu.
  • Open Sourcing of Fiori Generator Modules: The complete open-sourcing of these modules will provide greater flexibility and community involvement.
  • UX Renovation: A refreshed design for the Application Info Page to enhance usability and aesthetics.

If you want to try some of these yourself, please join us at our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on February 5, 2025.

What else could make your life as a developer easier? Please let us know in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: A Small Change for a Greener Tomorrow
entry.content:
Even if your statement about deforestation and oil industry is not wrong, saying ' It does not count at all' is wrong and quite agressive. 

An Amerindian legend tells that one day, in the Amazon rainforest, there was a huge fire. All the animals, terrified and helpless, watched the disaster. Only a small hummingbird was active. He would fetch drops of water one by one from the river and throw them on the fire with his beak. The armadillo, annoyed by this derisory action, ends up calling out to the hummingbird: "Aren't you crazy? Do you think you'll be able to put out the fire with that? » And the hummingbird replied, "No, but I'm doing my part."

entry.title: Re: AES Encryption in ABAP
entry.content:
Kindly notice that this Blog post is not an official SAP post.

Up to now, there's no API available in ABAP systems for performing cryptographic operations (such as data encryption / decryption plus the required proper storage of the keys) since such are subject of export control regulations.

We consider to change that in the future (i.e. providing such APIs in conjunction with a proper key handling/storage); but in the moment I'm unable to provide an estimated availability date.

Kind regards,
Wolfgang Janzen
(Product Owner for the product area "ABAP Security")

entry.title: Shaping the Future: SAP Analytics Cloud as a Resilient Force in the AI-Driven Evolution of Business
entry.content:
The Artificial Intelligence Storm

In today’s rapidly evolving technological world, businesses face the dual challenge of staying competitive while navigating through disruptive trends like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). Many platforms face the risk of obsolescence as they struggle to keep pace with changing demands. Nevertheless, despite these transformation trends, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) continues to prove its relevance by adapting and integrating into a dynamic ecosystem and landscapes of the companies and benefits from a continuous commitment to improvement and adoption. SAC remains a vital tool for organizations that are seeking to derive value from their data, as it is core to SAP’s product portfolio focusing on driving future innovation.

Evolving with the AI Revolution

SAC’s Integration and Privileges in the AI Storm

AI has fundamentally shifted the way organizations approach business intelligence (BI) and analytics. Traditional dashboards and manual reports are being enhanced or even fully replaced by predictive and augmented analytics and conversational BI tools. Companies are increasingly demanding platforms capable not only of analyzing historical data but also of anticipating trends and suggesting actionable insights in real time.

SAP Analytics Cloud is not just adapting to these demands but thrives to represent such an environment itself. It embeds machine learning and AI-driven analytics directly into its core functionalities and as a result, empowers its users to go beyond descriptive analytics. For example, its predictive planning capabilities allow businesses to simulate scenarios and to plan for outcomes with the highest accuracy. SAC supports other wide range of functionalities, such as "augmented analytics," which enables  AI to guide users through complex datasets and highlight patterns

Apart from the functionalities, one of SAP Analytics Cloud’s strongest assets is its seamless integration into SAP’s product ecosystem. Businesses that are already leveraging SAP solutions—be it SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, or SAP Ariba—find that SAC fits naturally into their workflows. This integration ensures a unified data landscape where users can access real-time insights without additional challenges or fragmented datasets.

SAC acts as a bridge between on-premises and cloud environments, allowing businesses to adopt hybrid analytics strategies. This flexibility is particularly critical for companies in industries with strict compliance requirements or those undergoing cloud transformations.

By remaining deeply embedded within the SAP portfolio, SAC reinforces its relevance. As organizations adopt new AI-driven tools and methodologies, SAC’s ability to integrate with emerging technologies ensures it evolves in parallel with the broader business environment.

Furthermore, SAP’s roadmap for SAC emphasizes agility, with regular updates and new features designed to align with emerging trends. This ensures that customers not only have access to state-of-the-art analytics but also a sense of security that their investment will remain relevant in the years to come. SAC is a dynamic platform, built to evolve and adjust in harmony with its users' needs.

Looking Ahead

The future of analytics is undeniably intertwined with Artificial Intelligence. Yet, the need for robust, reliable, and integrated BI tools remains unchanged. SAP Analytics Cloud exemplifies how a platform can adapt to technological shifts while remaining a dependable solution for businesses.

As the AI era progresses, SAC will continue to evolve, delivering actionable insights, fostering collaboration, and driving business outcomes. For organizations seeking a future-proof BI solution, SAC’s deep integration with SAP products and commitment to innovation makes it a natural choice

By focusing on integration, usability, and technological evolution, SAP Analytics Cloud is poised to remain at the forefront of analytics innovation, ensuring that businesses can thrive in an ever-changing world.

 

Andrii Pogorielov, Seda Agasarjan

Customer Services & Delivery MEE BT&A CFO Advisory

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: Hi Navi, my understanding: you are in a S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. You want to create a custom field with a value help showing Product data and as description field you want to use the description field of an already existing key user custom field. Correct? And you tried to created your own C1 released developer extensibility CDS view and you want to use this view as value help view for the new key user custom field. Correct? If this is correct then your assumption is correct. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition it is not possible to utilize a C1 released developer extensibility CDS view as value help view for a key user custom field. You can use key user Custom CDS views as value help view for a key user custom field...but you can not use a C1 released developer extensibility CDS view as value help view for a key user custom field. Anyway: maybe I still did not fully get your context. Maybe let us continue via email in this case: g.wilhelm@sap.com. Best regards Georg

entry.title: Is it possible to self approve a Sales Order?
entry.content:
Dear SAP, 

I am the approver of the workflow for sales orders, so if I create a sales order, can I approve it myself?

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to self approve a Sales Order?
entry.content:
Hello,

The short answer is no. The system does not allow the sales document creator to approve their own approval requests.

If there is other approver for this same workflow and you create the sales document, it will be automatically send to the other approver that is not you.

If you are the only approver for this workflow, the document will not be send to any approver. You can find this document and forward it to other approver on the app "Work Items Without Agents".

Check more information on the SAP Note 3473042 - Message "No Users Found For Task" Triggered in Sales Document Workflow.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Business Application Studio-Fiori Tools - Fiori app g...
entry.content:
Hi @cesar_gr, are you able to use the workaround that was documented above? i.e.:

 

  1. In SAP Business Application Studio, select `Terminal -> New Terminal` from the menu options.
  2. In the new terminal, type `npm i -g @SAP/generator-fiori@1.15.7`.  This will install the previous version of the SAP Fiori generator without this bug.
  3. Then please refresh your browser.

This should unblock you.  Please know that we are hoping to provide a hotfix update for this issue as soon as we can.

Regards,

entry.title: DS Analytic Model oData: all/any value for parameters
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an Analytic Model that has  some variables/parameters. When I preview the data only the START_DATE and END_DATE are mandatory. However, when I'm using the oData URL Generator to create URL for data consumption, I'm prompted to fill out all possible variables (see screenshot). 

Question 1: Why is that I need to pass a value for all variables?

Question 2: What can I put to signify all/any value (equivalent to leaving a variable blank in the Data Preview selection prompt)? For example how can I pass a selection for all values for 0PLANT?

entry.title: Re: API to read data from Manage Workforce app
entry.content:
Hello KhaingZinTun,

I think tehre is no such API available currently. You cannot read employee or work agreement masterdata. There are APIs for some HR objects, like timesheet or skills, but that's it.

You could browse the S/4HANA cloud roadmap and check if new APIs are announced. I have seen some roadmap items dealing with workforce replication and SAP MDM, maybe this can already help.

Regards,

Johannes

entry.title: Re: Communication arrangement SAP_COM_0301
entry.content:
Hi Christian,

You can use API-based creation of workforce or Excel-Upload in parallel. If I understand you correctly, you could import employees with SAP_COM_0301 from your HR system. And the contingent workers you could import via Excel templates. 

You mention there is no "customer master data" available, do you mean supplier master data? I assume for the contingent worker you rather need the relation BBP005 to a supplier.

Cheers, Johannes
